name: Dict Comprehensions
description: Questions on dict comprehensions and key-value building.

questions.question: {x: x**2 for x in range(4)} produces:
questions.explanation: Keys are 0,1,2,3 and values are their squares: 0,1,4,9.

questions.question: To swap keys and values of a dict d:
choices: {v: k for k, v in d.items()}
{k: v for v, k in d}
questions.explanation: Dict comprehension with v: k iterating over d.items() swaps key and value.

questions.question: {k: v for k, v in d.items() if v > 0} keeps:
questions.explanation: The if clause filters to only (k,v) pairs where v > 0.

questions.question: Building a dict from two lists keys and values:
questions.explanation: zip(keys, values) pairs them; dict() accepts an iterable of (k,v) pairs.

questions.question: {(x, x+1): x for x in [1,2]} has keys:
questions.explanation: Keys are (1,2) and (2,3); values are 1 and 2. Tuples are valid keys.
